query:
Please correct the proposed fix documentation with the following, then proceed: Site 2 collapse — combining the two if blocks into one predicate is the right  
  call. The current code has an implicit ordering dependency (empty check before
  equality check) that obscures the intent. The single predicate !src.is_empty() 
  && !tgt.is_empty() && src == tgt makes the semantics explicit.  

  Site 3 — the architect's note — the subtle point is right but slightly         
  mis-stated. Both-empty pairs don't "pass Site 3 today" — they'd be blocked by
  it ("" != "" is false → guard returns false → edge blocked). The accurate      
  framing is: both-empty pairs never reach Site 3 today because Sites 1 and 2
  filter them first. After Sites 1 and 2 are relaxed, Site 3 will see both-empty
  for the first time. That's why the fix is needed there — it's a newly-reachable
   code path, not a correction to existing behavior. The implementer needs to
  understand this or the Site 3 change will look unnecessary.

  The 4 test variants — correct coverage. The both-empty case is the most        
  important new one to add since it's the newly-reachable path through Site 3.
  Make sure the existing same-non-empty-blocked test is preserved and still      
  passes after the refactor.                                      

  One thing missing from the fix description: the source_feature_cycle field     
  comment on InformsCandidate (line 83) should be updated to say "empty string
  means pre-attribution entry; Informs detection allows this" rather than        
  implying a non-empty invariant. Otherwise a future agent reading that struct
  definition will re-introduce the old guard.

entries.title: Cross-feature guard over-restriction: is_empty() OR blocks entries with unknown provenance
ADR-002: edges_of_type as Sole Traversal Boundary for suppress_contradicts
ADR-005: No Config Toggle for Contradicts Suppression (col-030)
Multi-pass cleanup: all passes on the same reference table must apply identical status filters
